_comment: THE single machine-readable source of truth for Mental's supported platform matrix. Every consumer — core/build.gradle.kts task registration + plugin.yml api-version, scripts/integration-matrix.sh, .github/workflows/{build,release}.yml, the release-notes range — reads this file (jq / JsonSlurper). No Minecraft version or JDK literal may live anywhere else. Entries are version-sorted ascending, so entries[0] is the support floor (the legacy backport floor 1.9.4) and entries[-1] is the ceiling — the release-notes range and floor derivations read positionally. Paper entries drive the full era suites; the folia entry (platform: folia, suites: combat-smoke) boots a real Folia server and runs the boot suite + the Folia combat smoke. suites = full | boot | combat-smoke; a 'boot' entry runs only the classload/boot-safety suite. The Java-version-bypass server-flag story is DEAD: Mental ships a Multi-Release mega-jar whose base tree is class-v52 (Java 8), so every legacy Paper build now runs on the NEWEST Java it boots FLAGLESS — no outdated-Java advisories, maximal JVM performance (GC/JIT gains). 1.9.4–1.12.2 have no Java version guard and boot even on Java 25, but Java 24+ prints the JEP-472 native-access / sun.misc.Unsafe deprecation warning walls, so their newest warning-free rung is Java 21. The hard-cap builds run at their measured newest-clean-flagless rung (probed live 2026-07-03): 1.13.2 → 13 (14 and 15 both refuse with 'Only up to Java 12'), 1.15.2 → 14 (its guard names 14 as the cap), 1.16.5 → 16 (its own JvmChecker asks for exactly 16). bytecodeTier = the Multi-Release tier the entry's plugin loader × JVM will ACTUALLY read out of the mega jar, asserted live by the tester (-Dmental.tester.tier) so which tree loaded is a per-version FACT, never an assumption. The loader MR map settles each value: 1.9.4/1.10.2/1.11.2 have URLClassLoader-delegating plugin loaders (no JarFile of their own) that open the jar runtime-versioned on Java 9+, so on Java 21 they read the ORIGINAL versions/17 tree (major 61); 1.12.2/1.13.2/1.15.2 have plain new JarFile(File) loaders that never runtime-version, so they read the base tree (major 52) on ANY JVM; 1.16.5 IS MR-aware (reflective, Java 9+) but the jar carries no versioned tier ≤ Java 16 (versions/17 sits above it and jvmdg 1.3.6 cannot co-produce a versions/16 tier alongside it), so it too reads base (52); every 1.17.1+ paper and the folia entry reads versions/17 (major 61) — byte-identical to the 2.3.x line. NOTE: 1.14.4 is a KNOWN HOLE in the range — its terminal Paper build hard-caps at Java 13 with no IgnoreJavaVersion escape, so a Java-17-classfile plugin cannot load on any 1.14.4 server on a modern JVM (see docs/superpowers/research/2026-07-02-legacy-boot-viability.md); it is deliberately absent.

ocm._comment: The pinned kernitus OldCombatMechanics release the coexistence gate stages. CI (stageOcmJar) downloads url, verifies sha256, and stages to run/ocm-jar/OldCombatMechanics.jar. A locally-present jar there (e.g. a fork build) overrides the download. The OCM coexistence gate runs on the fixed 1.17.1 + 26.1.2 pair (its scope is the OCM ownership split, unchanged by the legacy backport) — pinned by version in the build, not positionally.
